description: Default three-tier E2E RAG query set. These cases are intentionally mixed: definition, distinction, association, ontology/graph reasoning, architecture, and exact-token retrieval.

github_reference_stack.name: Ragas
github_reference_stack.why: RAG evaluation patterns, test data generation, and app-level eval loops.

github_reference_stack.name: DeepEval
github_reference_stack.why: Black-box LLM/RAG E2E eval framing and regression-test style assertions.

github_reference_stack.name: Arize Phoenix
github_reference_stack.why: Trace-first observability and retrieval/response eval comparison.

github_reference_stack.name: Qdrant RAG Eval
github_reference_stack.why: Route comparison across naive, hybrid, reranked, and evaluated RAG pipelines.

github_reference_stack.name: Qdrant Improving R in RAG Workshop
github_reference_stack.why: Hybrid retrieval, sparse/dense representations, reranking, and MMR/diversity patterns.

queries.query: what is python and is ai essentially python
queries.why: Tests exact conceptual distinction and graph evidence that Python and AI are separate entities with a usage relationship.
expected_atoms: Python as an AI/ML implementation tool

queries.query: natural language processing and data augmentation
queries.why: Tests whether each tier can find the NLP/data-augmentation passage instead of generic synthetic-data hits.
expected_atoms: Relationship between augmentation and NLP training

queries.why: Tests whether retrieval can answer a broad ontology question from corpus evidence even when the query is not an exact title.
expected_atoms: Ontology as structured concepts and relations

queries.query: how do vector search, hybrid search, and graph augmentation work together in rag
queries.why: Tests the app's own retrieval-stack corpus knowledge and whether all route names map to the intended backend behavior.

queries.query: why can dense retrieval miss exact tokens and how does bm25 help
queries.why: Tests lexical evidence and sparse-vs-dense reasoning. Hybrid should beat dense-only when exact terms matter.
